---
title: 'catalog embed-readonly'
---

Embed read-only object-store credentials into the catalog's git-annex branch
so consumers cloning the catalog can fetch annexed content without supplying
credentials of their own.

Before embedding, the command probes the bucket with the supplied credentials
by initiating a multipart upload. If that write succeeds the credentials are
not read-only and the command raises an error instead of embedding them.

Embedding sets `embedcreds=yes` on the S3 remote config and writes the result
to `remote.log` on the git-annex branch.

## Usage

```bash
xorq catalog embed-readonly [OPTIONS]
```

## Options

| Option | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--env-file` | none | Env file containing the read-only credentials. Mutually exclusive with `--env-prefix`. |
| `--env-prefix` | none | Env-var prefix for the read-only credentials. Mutually exclusive with `--env-file`. |
| `--gcs` | off | Apply GCS defaults to the S3 remote config. |

One of `--env-file` or `--env-prefix` is required.

## Examples

```bash
xorq catalog embed-readonly --env-file .env.catalog.readonly
xorq catalog embed-readonly --env-prefix XORQ_CATALOG_RO_
```

## Notes

- The write probe requires the credentials to be evaluated against the
  configured bucket; misconfigured paths will surface as setup errors before
  any embed occurs.
- The probe initiates (and aborts) a multipart upload—credentials that grant
  write access on the bucket will be detected even if no object is left behind.
